scenarios.query:
Audit this enforcement-apparatus scan.
```text
suppressions:
  src/legacy/parser.js:1   /* eslint-disable */        (file-wide, no rule list, no reason)
  src/api/users.py:42      # noqa                       (bare, no code, no reason)
  src/api/orders.py:88     # type: ignore               (bare)
lint baseline (detekt): 1,204 entries, up from 1,011 three months ago
unused: 17 eslint-disable directives report as unused; 9 mypy ignores flagged by warn_unused_ignores
```
expected_behavior: Flags the file-wide `/* eslint-disable */` and the bare `# noqa` / `# type: ignore` as blanket suppressions — scope each to a named rule/error code and add a reason
Flags the baseline growing (1,011 → 1,204) as debt accretion — a ratchet should shrink, not grow; track the count as a trend
Flags the 17 unused eslint-disable and 9 unused mypy ignores as stale, masking the next real violation at those spots — remove them
Frames suppression as opting out of enforcement and recommends rule-scoped + justified (issue link / expiry) suppressions

scenarios.query:
Audit these alert rules and dashboards.
```text
alerts:
  HighCPU:          expr cpu_pct > 0.8   for: (none)   annotations: {}        # cause-based, no runbook, no `for`
  DiskWillFill6h:   expr predict_linear(...) < 0   for: 15m   runbook: https://wiki/runbooks/disk   severity: page  # ok
dashboards: 3 panels on "API health" query metric `http_requests_total_v1` (renamed to _v2 last quarter)
monitoring is configured by hand in the Grafana/Datadog UI; not in version control
```
expected_behavior: Flags HighCPU as cause-based and unactionable (no runbook, no `for:`, no labels) — alert on a user-visible symptom / SLO burn, not a raw CPU gauge
Recommends running a rule linter (pint / promtool) and adding `for:` + severity + a runbook annotation
Flags the 3 dashboard panels querying the renamed/dead `http_requests_total_v1` metric as drift giving false confidence
Flags click-ops monitoring (not in version control) and recommends monitoring-as-code; recognizes DiskWillFill6h (symptom + runbook + for) as healthy

scenarios.query:
Audit generated-artifact freshness.
```text
checked-in generated: api/openapi_client/ (from openapi.yaml), db/queries.go (sqlc from queries.sql)
CI: lints + builds + tests; no regenerate-and-diff step
history: openapi.yaml changed in 6 commits since api/openapi_client/ was last regenerated
generated files have no `linguist-generated` / generated-by header
```
expected_behavior: Flags the absence of a `regenerate && git diff --exit-code` CI gate, so generated artifacts can silently drift from their source/spec
Flags the concrete drift signal — openapi.yaml changed in 6 commits since the client was last regenerated
Recommends a CI job that regenerates (openapi + sqlc) and fails on any diff
Flags that generated files are unmarked (no linguist-generated/header), so a human may hand-edit what the next regen clobbers

scenarios.query:
Audit this enforcement-apparatus scan.
```text
suppressions: all rule-scoped with reason + issue link (e.g. `# noqa: E501  # long external URL, see #221`); baseline shrinking 420 → 380 over the quarter; 0 unused (RUF100 / warn_unused_ignores clean)
alerts: all symptom/SLO-based, each links a runbook, `pint` passes in CI
codegen: CI runs `make generate && git diff --exit-code`; passes; generated files marked linguist-generated
```
expected_behavior: Reports no findings — suppressions are scoped, justified, and shrinking; alerts are symptom-based with runbooks and lint-clean; codegen drift is gated in CI
Confirms the enforcement apparatus is healthy rather than inventing issues
Does not manufacture findings on a clean scan
